citations.authors: Atluri S, Ragkousi K, Cortezzo DE, Setlow P
citations.title: Bacillus subtilis spore germination: evidence for multiple nutrient receptors

amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Spore germination in Bacillus subtilis is the developmental process by which a dormant, resistant endospore returns to vegetative growth. Germination is triggered by nutrient germinants (L-alanine, AGFK sugars) binding to germinant receptors (GerA, GerB, GerK), leading to irreversible commitment through cortex hydrolysis, core rehydration, and metabolic reactivation. Multiple receptors provide redundancy and environmental sensing.

name: Spore Germination

sources.title: The Genetics of Spore Germination in Bacillus subtilis
The Bacillus subtilis gerB locus encodes a germinant receptor and is a cluster of three cotranscribed genes
Bacterial Spore Germination: Recent Advances and Comments on the State of the Art
